title: Stachygynandrum laevigatum
intro: Stachygynandrum laevigatum, also known as Korean mistletoe or Korean gall, is a type of parasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host plant, typically the Korean pine (Pinus koraiensis). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Stachygynandrum laevigatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or extract, and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
